title: National Framework for qPCR-Based Authentication in Food Safety
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The   authentication     of     animal-derived     food     products     is     critical     to     ensure     consumer     trust  ,   protect     public     health  ,   and     prevent     food     fraud  .   Conventional     methods     such   as   microscopy     or   protein-  based     tests   are   time-consuming     and     prone     to     inaccuracies  ,   especially   in   processed     products  . Real-  time   PCR (  qPCR  )   offers     a     highly     sensitive  ,   specific  ,   and     rapid     method     for     verifying     species     identity     at     the   DNA level.   Implementing     a     national     framework     for     qPCR-based     authentication     will     standardize     protocols  ,   improve     laboratory     capacity  ,   and     enable     consistent  ,   reliable     results     across     the     food     supply     chain  .   This     policy     brief     highlights     the     need     for     a     coordinated     strategy  ,   integrating     qPCR     into     food     safety     monitoring     systems     to     enhance     traceability  ,   ensure     regulatory     compliance  ,   and     strengthen     the     integrity     of     domestic     and     export     markets  .
